title: 2D and 3D Scans of Granular Rock Material Heaps and Calibration Models

paper_analysis.summary: This Scientific Data paper presents a dataset of 2D photographs and 3D photogrammetric scans of 86 granular rock heaps (dolomite, quartz sand, marble, gravel), plus one physical calibration cone and four CAD cone models, all intended for angle-of-repose (AoR) measurement and method calibration. The key contribution is a standardized, openly shared set of raw images and derived 2D/3D models (edge coordinates, point clouds, STL files) that enables comparison of AoR measurement methods and validation against known-geometry references.
measurement_types: 2D digital image analysis of heap profiles
calibration measurements on a physical cone

figures.description: Lifting-cylinder heap formation testing rig used to create granular heaps with constrained vertical motion.
Example heap sample formed on the test plate.
Workflow for 2D digital image analysis: image acquisition, thresholding/edge detection, and extraction of contour coordinates.
Workflow for creating a 3D model using photogrammetry from multiple photographs.
Data structure for samples used in 3D analysis, showing raw photographs and derived point-cloud/STL outputs plus marker coordinates.
Data structure for samples used in 2D analysis, showing raw photographs and derived edge-detection outputs and edge coordinates.
Calibrated regular cone coated with dolomite grains, used as a reference body for validation.
Example real granular heap used to illustrate non-ideal heap morphology.
Line fitting on a 2D extracted model cross-section for estimating cone/heap slope and R2.
3D cone fitting on the reconstructed point cloud, including point-cloud distance deviations from the ideal cone.

paper_analysis.raw_measurement_details: Raw measurements are photographs of heap samples and reference objects. For 2D analysis, raw data are two level photographs taken from perpendicular directions; for 3D analysis, raw data are multiple angled photographs (at least 10 for t1, 18/33/108/5 depending on variant) taken around the heap. The dataset also includes raw CAD geometry files for the synthetic cone models, not photographs.

dataset_characterization.data_availability_statement: The dataset is available at a Figshare repository (doi:10.6084/m9.figshare.28468526). Data records are organized in .rar archives by material, with subdirectories for each sample testing. The paper states that raw photographs, processed 2D/3D outputs, and CAD-derived models are included.
dataset_characterization.raw_data_description: Raw data consist of photographs taken with a Pentax K5 16.3 MP camera and Pentax SMC DA 18–55 mm lens. For 2D, two horizontal images per sample are taken against a black background; for 3D, multiple photographs are taken around the heap or on a turntable. For CAD samples, the input is CAD geometry rather than photographs.
dataset_characterization.processed_data_description: Processed data include 2D edge-detection rasters and contour/edge coordinates stored in text files, and 3D point-cloud and STL files generated with Agisoft PhotoScan. Marker point coordinate files are also included for 3D samples to define the reference coordinate system. The paper also reports validation tables with fitted angles, base diameters, R2 values, and point-cloud standard deviations.
dataset_characterization.figure_data_description: Yes. The paper explicitly describes figure-related data structures for 2D and 3D samples and shows example analysis outputs in figures (e.g., line fitting and cone fitting). However, no separate figure source-data files are explicitly mentioned beyond the dataset contents and repository organization.
dataset_characterization.scripts_description: No custom code or scripts were used. The paper states that specialized software was used: Agisoft PhotoScan v1.4.1, NIH ImageJ v1.52, CloudCompare v2.13.1, and MS Excel for basic statistics. No notebooks or analysis scripts are mentioned.
dataset_characterization.notes: The dataset mixes experimental raw images, derived geometric models, and synthetic reference geometries. The 3D data include both point clouds and STL meshes; the 2D data include edge-detection rasters and extracted coordinates. The CAD models are not measured images but theoretical geometries exported directly to point clouds/STL/cross-sections. Repeated measurements exist for repeatability testing, especially for the calibration cone and selected materials. The paper emphasizes that the raw photographs allow users to rebuild and independently validate the models.

classification_prior.raw_data_evidence: The Data Records section says archives contain 'raw data (photographs taken)' for both 2D and 3D models, and CAD geometry for synthetic models.
classification_prior.source_data_evidence: The same section states that directories also contain 'edge detection raster', 'built 2D models in form of edge coordinates', and for 3D 'Point-cloud and STL files' plus marker coordinates.
classification_prior.data_availability_section_relevant: “The dataset is available at Figshare repository”; “archives contain directories”; “raw data (photographs taken)”; “edge detection raster”; “built 2D models in form of edge coordinates”; “3D model in form of Point-cloud and STL files”; “marker points coordinates file”; “No custom code has been used”.
